venue: ACL 2024 (original); follow-up 'Multi-Agent Collaboration via Evolving Orchestration' at NeurIPS 2025
execution_kernel_pattern: Chat Chain (v1.x): a directed, phase-decomposed pipeline (design -> coding -> testing -> documenting) where each phase is a two-agent role-play dialogue with a fixed instructor/assistant pair; output of one phase is input to the next. v2.0 (DevAll, 2026-01) replaces the hardcoded software-company waterfall with a YAML-configurable workflow canvas + visual DAG editor, generalizing the chain to arbitrary multi-agent orchestration.

multi_agent_support.style: role-play with fixed personas (CEO, CTO, programmer, reviewer, tester, art designer); two-agent dialogue per phase, chained by chat chain
multi_agent_support.notes: v1.x is tightly scoped to a waterfall software-company SOP. v2.0 generalizes to user-defined roles via YAML templates (data analysis, game dev, video generation, 3D via Blender). Adds 'Experiential Co-Learning' (ECL) and 'Iterative Experience Refinement' (IER) for cross-task experience accumulation.

tool_protocol: v1.x: limited, mostly internal (file write, code execution sandbox). v2.0: integrates Model Context Protocol (MCP) for external tools, plus first-class connectors for Blender (3D), Manim (video), and OpenClaw orchestration runtime.
memory_model: Per-phase chat history + 'memory stream' across phases (v1.x). ECL/IER adds a persistent experience pool of (task, shortcut, outcome) tuples that future runs retrieve and refine — closer to a case-based-reasoning memory than vector RAG.
supported_model_providers: Provider-agnostic via generic API_KEY + BASE_URL config. Originally GPT-3.5/4 via OpenAI; community configs for Claude, local OSS models via OpenAI-compatible endpoints (Ollama, vLLM).
observability_story: v1.x: visualized chat logs + replay viewer (WebUI). v2.0: real-time logging in web console, human-in-the-loop interruption, visual DAG execution monitor. No native OpenTelemetry/tracing emit — observability is bespoke UI, not a standards-based hook.
typical_use_case: v1.x: end-to-end auto-generation of small/medium software projects from a one-line natural-language prompt (the viral 2023-2024 demo). v2.0: general 'developing everything' — data viz, 3D scenes, short videos, research drafts — via configurable agent teams.
maturity_signal: ACTIVE and academically credible. 33.2k stars; v2.2.0 released 2026-03; NeurIPS 2025 follow-up paper ('Evolving Orchestration'). The 2023-2024 viral moment did NOT lead to abandonment — instead the team executed a deliberate v1->v2 pivot in Jan 2026 from software-only to general orchestration platform. Strong publication record (ACL 2024 + NeurIPS 2025) backs the engineering.
one_paragraph_differentiator: vs MetaGPT: both are role-play 'software company' frameworks, but ChatDev's orchestration unit is the Chat Chain (a sequence of two-agent phase dialogues) whereas MetaGPT encodes SOPs as structured artifact handoffs (PRD -> design doc -> code) with each role producing a typed document. ChatDev leans on natural-language chat as the primary medium with 'communicative dehallucination' (the listener proactively asks targeted questions before acting) to suppress error propagation; MetaGPT relies on schema-typed messages. vs CrewAI: CrewAI is a generic role/task DSL with no built-in SOP — you bring the process; ChatDev v1 ships a hardcoded waterfall and v2 ships a visual workflow studio. vs AutoGen: AutoGen is a conversation-graph runtime (any-to-any group chat, programmable termination); ChatDev is a vertically-integrated product with opinionated phases, UIs, and domain templates (3D, video, code).
known_anti_patterns: Output quality on non-trivial software tasks remains limited — the demos that went viral were small CLI tools, not production systems; independent reproductions (e.g. SWE-bench attempts) show ChatDev underperforms agentic coding tools (SWE-agent, OpenHands) on realistic bug-fix tasks. v1.x's hardcoded waterfall is rigid (poor fit for iterative/exploratory workflows). v2.0 'DevAll' marketing-heavy (zero-code, drag-and-drop) risks the hello-world-only trap until third-party benchmarks land. No native OTel observability. Provider config is loose (API_KEY/BASE_URL) but no first-class structured tool/function-calling abstraction across providers.
communicative_dehallucination_note: Mechanism described in ACL 2024 paper: before an assistant agent executes an instruction, it proactively decomposes and re-queries the instructor with clarifying sub-questions, exchanging multiple turns until the spec is concrete enough — empirically reduces downstream hallucination vs single-shot instruction-following.
chat_chain_note: Chat Chain = a chain of 'phases'; each phase is one (instructor, assistant) role-play dialogue with a phase-specific prompt template and termination condition. The chain is statically defined per task type (software-dev chain has 4 phases x ~3 sub-tasks). v2.0 makes the chain user-editable.
